title: Bio - hCANCO 3D

hNCOCX. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1qo7d","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"vgre","text":"Checklist previous standard experiment acquired using the same spectrometer, probe, and spinning rate (if exist).","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a7696","text":"Updated the optimized parameters (p14, plw14, p13, plw13, p15, plw15, p43, spnam41, cnst41, spnam31, cnst31, p35, spnam32, cnst32, spnam52, cnst52, p53, spnam33, cnst33, spnam53, cnst53, p38, spnam38, cnst38, cpdprg4, p24, and cnst24).","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fn2sg","text":"Set carrier frequencies for each channel (O1p, O2p, O3p, cnst16, guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"f5gcr","text":"This SOP is written based on the pulse sequence developed at NMRFAM. If using other pulse sequences, the strategy is still applicable, but the parameter names will be different. Please refer to the schematic pulse sequence at section 6.10 to find corresponding parameter names if using other pulse sequences ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"a23vo","text":"MAS rate: 13.333 kHz at 600 MHz, 16.667 kHz at 750 MHz, or 20 kHz at 900 MHz (~88.9 ppm in 13C).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9},{"style":"sup","offset":91,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2s7ka","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3legt","text":"Temperature:  As determined for optimal sample sensitivity and resolution.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":12}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2c7d6","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"3nul5","text":"Below optimizations are required before setting up the hCANCO3d experiment (parameters needs to be updated are shown in parenthesis). Example SOP for each optimization is attached at References.  Note that rf powers in this pulse sequence defined using kHz rather than Watt (i.e., input kHz number for rf powers and the code will calculate the corresponding Watt numbers for Topspin to use). Only the hard pulses for calibration need to be input as Watt (plw13, plw14, and plw15).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":32}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"frp39","text":"Calibration of 1H, 13C, and 15N solid pulses (p14, plw14, p13, plw13, p15, and plw15)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":15,"length":1},{"style":"sup","offset":19,"length":2},{"style":"sup","offset":28,"length":2}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"bn3tg","text":"Optimization of HCA CP (p43, spnam41, cnst41, spnam31, and cnst31)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7k7q8","text":"Optimization of CAN CP (p35, spnam32, cnst32, spnam52, and cnst52)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"84kdb","text":"Optimization of NCO CP (p53, spnam33, cnst33, spnam53, and cnst53)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"98dmv","text":"Optimization of CA selective pulse (p38, spnam38, cnst38)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c923l","text":"Optimization of high-power 1H decoupling (cpdprg4, p24, and cnst24)","type":"ordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":27,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8u836","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"4ku99","text":"Setup time:  ~15 min, presuming initial calibrations in the cited SOPs have already been completed.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":11}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"fdn71","text":"Operator:  User should be knowledgeable to operate MAS probes and use Topspin. User should know the power limit of the MAS probe being used.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Load pulse program and parameter set. “CNC3d-NAN” pulse program and parameter set “hCANCO3d-NAN_par”.

If a previous hCANCO3d experiment acquired using the same setup is available (i.e., same spectrometer,probe, pulse program and MAS rate), open the previous dataset and type “edc”. Input the directory for the new experiment and press “OK”.
If no previous data available, then use Topspin commend “edc” to open a new experiment. Input “CNC3d-NAN” as PULPROG. Type “rpar”, then load “hCANCO3d-NAN_par”.

Set the 1 H carrier frequency (O2p) at ~5 ppm, 13 C carrier frequency (O1p) at 175 ppm, and 15 N carrier frequency (O3p) at 118 ppm. For example, type “O1p” and enter, then input 175 to the opened window.

Optimize the pulse sequence parameters. The optimization needs to be done are listed in the "Before start" section under "Guidelines & Warnings". Example SOPs for the required optimization are attached at "References". Note that the parameters can be optimized use different methods/pulse sequences. Please consult to your facility manager to perform the optimization.
Set the carrier frequencies of 13 C channel.
Set cnst16 to 175 ppm. This constant is used as the frequency for detection and 3 rd NCO CP. Type “cnst16”, then input 175.
Set cnst17 to 55 ppm. This constant is used as the frequency for the 1 st hCA CP, 2 nd CAN CP, and the soft selective pulse for CA. Type “cnst17”, then input 55.
Set the acquisition parameters.

Direct dimension (t 3 or F3) Default acquisition time: depending on 13 C T 2 , default is ~20ms (alternative for 13 C-glycerol: ~30 ms). Default spectral width: ~300 ppm. Default carrier frequency: ~175 ppm.

Indirect 13 C dimension (t 1 or F1) Increment of delay (IN_F, s): n * τ r . For a given MAS rate, adjust the increment of delay to integer multiples of τ r that covers at least 30 ppm 13 C spectral width. Spectral width (SW, ppm): coupled with increment of delay, >30 ppm is required to cover the full spectrum Carrier frequency (O1p. ppm): 175 ppm. Hypercomplex scheme (FnMODE): States-TPPI Maximum evolution time (AQ, s): depending on 13 C T 2 , the default is ~10 ms. The evolution time equals to (Increment of delay) * (TD). Once Increment of time is fixed as described above, change number of TD to adjust the total evolution time.

Indirect 15 N dimension (t 2 or F2) Increment of delay (IN_F, s): n * τ r . For example, 375 ms can be used for 13.33 kHz MAS on 600 MHz. For a given MAS rate, adjust the Increment of delay to integer multiples of τ r that covers at least 40 ppm 15 N spectral width. Spectral width (SW, ppm): coupled with increment of delay, >40 ppm is required to cover the full spectrum. Carrier frequency (O3p, ppm): 118 ppm. Hypercomplex scheme (FnMODE): States-TPPI Maximum evolution time (AQ, s): depending on 15 N T 2 , the default is ~10 ms. The evolution time equals to (Increment of delay) * (TD). Once increment of delay is fixed as described above, change number of TD to adjust the total evolution time.
Set the recycle delay

Use recycle delay of 1.3*T 1 for maximum sensitivity per unit time. If 1 H T 1 is not measured, use the default value which is 2 s. However, measuring 1 H T 1 before setting this experiment is highly recommended.
Due to high-power 1 H decoupling is applied, don’t set recycle delay less than 1s.
Set NUS schedule. This step is optional, but 25% NUS is recommended for 3D experiments.
Adjust measurement time as required by incrementing number of scans in multiples of 16.
Validation
Start the experiment and monitor the first ~20-30 rows
Process first dimension FT to check for adequate signal correctly arraying indirect dimension.
creator: NMRFAM Facility
